data.title: Picasso Masterpiece Remains Hidden Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: A Picasso masterpiece, considered a precursor to the renowned painting 'Guernica,' remains stored in a basement in Tehran as conflict continues to affect Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the artwork's obscured status amid the ongoing turmoil. The painting's concealment underscores the broader impact of the conflict on cultural heritage and art preservation in the region. Despite its significance, the masterpiece remains inaccessible to the public, reflecting the challenges faced by cultural institutions in conflict zones.

data.title: Recognizing Common Scam Red Flags for Older Investors
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) posted on X, highlighting the importance of recognizing common red flags in scams targeting older investors. These scams often involve time-sensitive offers, high-pressure sales tactics, deals that seem too good to be true, and promises of high guaranteed returns. 

The SEC emphasizes the need for vigilance among older investors to protect themselves from fraudulent schemes. Understanding these warning signs can help individuals avoid falling victim to scams that exploit their financial security. 

Investors are encouraged to thoroughly research any investment opportunity and consult with trusted financial advisors before making decisions. By staying informed and cautious, older investors can safeguard their assets and ensure their financial well-being.

data.title: Significant SOL Transfer from Wintermute to Fireblocks Custody
data.description: A substantial transfer of SOL tokens has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 20:41 UTC+8, 55,432.01 SOL were moved from Wintermute to Fireblocks Custody.

data.title: The Enduring Challenge of Résumés in Job Hunting
data.description: Résumés have long been a staple in the job search process, but their effectiveness has been questioned for nearly a century. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that signs of their limited usefulness have been evident since the 1920s. Despite technological advancements and changes in hiring practices, résumés remain a central component of job applications, often causing frustration for job seekers and employers alike. As the job market evolves, the debate over the relevance of résumés continues, with some advocating for alternative methods of showcasing skills and experiences.

data.title: The United States claims it has destroyed Iranian facilities that threatened the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: In a video, U.S. Central Command Commander Brad Cooper stated that the United States bombed Iranian coastal facilities that threatened shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. He said the U.S. struck Iranian underground facilities and anti-ship missiles, as well as intelligence support systems and radar equipment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Sam Bankman-Fried Intensifies Praise for U.S. President Trump Amid Speculation
data.description: Sam Bankman-Fried, who is serving a 25-year prison sentence, has increased his public praise for U.S. President Donald Trump. According to ChainCatcher, Bankman-Fried expressed support for the recent U.S. military action against Iran, describing it as a necessary measure to address nuclear threats. He claimed that the operation significantly weakened Iran's military capabilities. His comments have sparked speculation about his intentions to seek a presidential pardon.

data.title: Vessels Linked to Iran, China, and Syria Navigate Hormuz Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: As the conflict progresses into its fourth week, Bloomberg posted on X that the Hormuz Tracker has identified a limited number of vessels associated with Iran, China, and Syria traversing the strategic waterway. The presence of these ships highlights the geopolitical tensions in the region, as the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical passage for global oil shipments. The situation continues to be closely monitored by international observers, given the potential implications for global energy markets and regional stability.

data.title: Russia Condemns Attack on Iran's Natanz Facility as Violation of International Law
data.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has condemned the recent attack on Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility, labeling it a blatant violation of international law. According to Jin10, the ministry emphasized the importance of adhering to international agreements and norms, urging all parties to exercise restraint and avoid actions that could escalate tensions in the region. The attack on the Natanz facility has raised concerns about regional stability and the potential for further conflict. Russia's statement reflects its stance on maintaining international legal standards and promoting diplomatic solutions to disputes.

data.title: Bahrain's King and Egypt's President Stress Importance of Halting Iranian Threats in Hormuz Strait
data.description: Bahrain's King and Egypt's President have emphasized the critical need to prevent Iranian threats from disrupting the freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. According to Jin10, the leaders highlighted the strategic significance of the waterway, which is a vital passage for global oil shipments. They called for collective efforts to ensure the security and stability of the region, underscoring the potential economic and geopolitical consequences of any interference in the strait. The Strait of Hormuz is a crucial maritime route, and its security is essential for maintaining the flow of energy resources worldwide.

data.title: U.S. Claims Destruction of Iranian Facilities Threatening Hormuz Strait
data.description: The United States has announced the destruction of Iranian facilities that posed a threat to the strategic Hormuz Strait. According to Jin10, this development highlights ongoing tensions in the region, which is a critical passage for global oil shipments. The U.S. military's actions are part of broader efforts to ensure the security of international shipping lanes. The Hormuz Strait remains a focal point of geopolitical interest, with various nations closely monitoring the situation to safeguard their energy supplies.

data.title: Modi Emphasizes Secure Shipping Lanes in Talks with Iran's President
data.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi highlighted the significance of maintaining open and secure shipping lanes during a conversation with Iran's President Ahmad Masoud Pezeshkian. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that the discussion underscored the strategic importance of maritime routes for regional and global trade. Modi's remarks come amid ongoing geopolitical tensions that could impact international shipping and trade dynamics. The dialogue between the two leaders reflects a mutual interest in ensuring the stability and security of these vital maritime corridors.

data.title: Thailand Considers Reducing Visa-Free Stay to 30 Days for Foreign Tourists
data.description: Thailand is contemplating a reduction in the visa-free stay period for foreign tourists from 60 days to 30 days. According to Ming Pao, this decision follows reports of foreign visitors engaging in illegal work or scams after entering the country without a visa. The visa policy committee, comprising the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and other relevant agencies, reviewed the current policy and concluded that the 60-day visa-free period exceeds typical tourism needs.

data.title: HSBC: Maintains expectation that the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates unchanged for the next two years.
data.description: HSBC stated that the Federal Reserve kept its policy rate unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% at its March meeting, hinting at a "wait-and-see" approach. Persistent inflation and rising geopolitical risks have created uncertainty for the Fed. We maintain our previous view that the Fed will keep rates unchanged in 2026 and 2027. Inflation risks have increased, particularly due to soaring energy prices, while labor market risks have slightly decreased. Energy price volatility and geopolitical risks should continue to support safe-haven demand and a stronger dollar. (Jinshi)

data.title: Former Illinois Bank Manager Permanently Barred by U.S. Federal Reserve
data.description: The U.S. Federal Reserve has permanently barred former Illinois bank manager Sandra Adams following accusations of theft. According to NS3.AI, Adams allegedly stole over $20,000 from an elderly customer's account and from the bank itself. The misconduct reportedly occurred between November 2023 and July 2024, leading to her permanent exclusion from the banking industry.

data.title: Trump Administration Proposes New Emissions Regulations
data.description: The Trump administration has introduced a proposal to modify emissions regulations, aligning with its broader strategy to reduce environmental restrictions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the administration's efforts to ease regulations on a larger scale. This move is part of U.S. President Donald Trump's ongoing agenda to promote economic growth by reducing regulatory burdens on industries. The proposal aims to balance environmental concerns with economic interests, sparking debate among policymakers and environmental groups. Critics argue that the changes could undermine efforts to combat climate change, while supporters believe it will boost industrial productivity and job creation. The administration's approach reflects its commitment to prioritizing economic development over stringent environmental policies.

data.title: AI and Inflation Discussed on Wall Street Week
data.description: David Westin hosted a discussion on inflation and artificial intelligence during the latest edition of Wall Street Week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting insights from Randal Quarles and Professor Arvind Narayanan of Princeton. Quarles, a former U.S. Federal Reserve official, shared his perspectives on the current inflation trends and their implications for the economy. Meanwhile, Narayanan provided an analysis of AI developments and their potential impact on various sectors. The conversation aimed to shed light on these critical issues affecting the financial markets and broader economic landscape.

data.title: Iran Resumes Natural Gas Supply to Iraq at 5 Million Cubic Meters Daily
data.description: Iran has resumed its natural gas supply to Iraq, delivering a daily volume of 5 million cubic meters, according to Jin10. This development was reported by the Iraqi National News Agency, highlighting the ongoing energy cooperation between the two neighboring countries. The resumption of gas supply is expected to support Iraq's energy needs and strengthen bilateral relations.

data.title: Cesar Chavez's Legacy: A Reflection on Power and Corruption
data.description: Cesar Chavez, a prominent figure in social movements, is remembered for his contributions to labor rights. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities of his legacy, which serves as a reminder of how power can corrupt even the most noble causes. Chavez's influence in advocating for farm workers' rights remains significant, but his leadership also faced criticism over time. The discussion around Chavez's impact continues to evolve, reflecting on both his achievements and the challenges within his movement.

data.title: Sources say the US troop buildup in the Middle East is intended to "open up" the Strait of Hormuz or seize islands.
data.description: On March 20, local time, US officials stated that the US military is deploying three additional warships and approximately 2,500 Marines to the Middle East. Previously, informed US sources indicated that the troop increase would provide President Trump with more military options, including launching an operation to "open up" the Strait of Hormuz, which would require deploying air and naval forces to the Iranian coastline. According to sources, the Trump administration is also considering deploying ground troops to Kharg Island, a vital artery for Iranian oil exports. This plan aims to seize the island and use it as leverage to force Iran to restore access to the Strait of Hormuz. Kharg Island, located in the northwestern Persian Gulf, more than 20 kilometers from the Iranian coast, is Iran's largest crude oil export base, with approximately 90% of Iran's oil exported from there. (CCTV)

data.title: OpenSea Delays TGE, Aster Chain Launches Mainnet, Tempo Goes Live, Tally Shuts Down
data.description:
OpenSea has announced a delay in its Token Generation Event (TGE), impacting its timeline for token distribution. According to NS3.AI, this postponement comes as the platform reassesses its strategic priorities.

In other developments, Aster Chain has successfully launched its mainnet and introduced staking capabilities, marking a significant milestone for the blockchain network. This launch is expected to enhance the platform's functionality and attract more users.

Meanwhile, Tempo has officially gone live, offering new services to its users. The platform aims to provide innovative solutions in the digital space, expanding its reach and capabilities.

On a different note, Tally has decided to shut down after six years of operation. The decision to cease operations reflects the challenges faced in maintaining sustainability in a competitive market environment.
